nonBlockingIssues.highlights: Unused import (os), unused constant (NY_TZ), dead variables (start_ts/end_ts), unused calendar_days field, silent exception suppression in params snapshot, hardcoded EURUSD pair
Content-Length defaults to 0 edge case, query string in path not handled, suppressed GET request logs
Linear scan O(n*m) in nearest-candle-time lookup, GT CSS in shared validate.html, marker deduplication collapses same-time same-primitive detections
Unawaited save call (safe via last-writer-wins), dead resetValidateGT function, unescaped innerHTML for params (low risk), silent save failures, redundant label fetch in validate-app.js

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add Python code hygiene rules alongside existing JS rules: remove unused imports, remove dead variables/constants, prefer logging warnings over bare 'except: pass'. Currently the Code Hygiene section only covers JS conventions (underscore prefix, init guards, Plotly.purge).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cli-batch-generator left unused 'import os', unused NY_TZ constant, dead start_ts/end_ts variables, and uses 'except Exception: pass' in site/detect.py. AGENTS.md Code Hygiene section only covers JS patterns.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the typeof guard pattern for cross-file function calls in vanilla JS architecture. Workers should use `if (typeof fnName === 'function') fnName()` when calling functions defined in other script files to maintain loose coupling. This pattern was discovered and applied consistently by multiple workers but is not documented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: validate-chart.js uses typeof guards at lines 183, 358 for initValidateGT/onVGTWeekChange/rebuildVGTRings. validate-app.js uses same pattern at lines 428, 435, 442. Both workers independently adopted this pattern without explicit guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Note in ra-validation-worker skill: agent-browser click command may time out on nested card/link elements with complex child structures. Workaround: use eval-based JS clicks (e.g., document.querySelector('a.card-link').click()) instead of direct click commands on such elements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: index-integration-and-e2e worker reported click timeouts on card elements with nested divs, required eval-based JS clicks as workaround. Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges confirms this pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml generate_validation_data command was correctly registered (cli-batch-generator review)
rejectedObservations.reason: Not an issue — observation confirms correct behavior, no action needed

rejectedObservations.observation: validate.html provides DOM scaffolding (CSS + HTML elements) for validate-gt.js feature within the validate-page-and-chart commit
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a reasonable architectural choice for a shared host page. The feature descriptions explicitly state validate.html is the container page and GT is a downstream feature. No documentation change needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: validate-gt.js is 681 lines, larger than handoff reported (~470 lines)
rejectedObservations.reason: File size is within reasonable bounds for a vanilla JS module handling click-to-label, ring primitives, lock panel, and export. No guideline needed — this is not a recurring pattern requiring action.

rejectedObservations.observation: toTS() function in validate-app.js adds timezone offset stripping beyond CHART_PATTERNS_REFERENCE.md
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a valid adaptation for Phase 3.5 data format. CHART_PATTERNS_REFERENCE.md is a Phase 3 document specific to Mode 1. Phase 3.5 has its own data formats documented in AGENTS.md Data Format Specifications section.

rejectedObservations.observation: Transcript skeleton for worker session 7dc34baf has 0 tool calls despite extensive verification
rejectedObservations.reason: Transcript logging gap — not within scope of scrutiny validation. This is a platform concern, not a codebase/guidance issue.
